title: GroundControl bootstrap checklist
description: Verify every core integration is installed and reachable on the active VPS.

steps.title: Docker is installed
steps.content: GroundControl manages containers through Docker. Verify the daemon is reachable from the host.
steps.aiHint: If docker is missing, install it from Services → Install or run the bootstrap routine.

steps.title: Git is installed
steps.content: Git is used to clone repositories during builds and deploys.
steps.aiHint: Install git from Services → Install if this check fails.

steps.title: Caddy is installed and running
steps.content: Caddy is the default edge reverse proxy. It should be installed and managed by systemd or openrc.
steps.aiHint: If caddy is installed but not active, run 'systemctl start caddy' or check the Caddyfile syntax.

steps.title: Nginx is available
steps.content: Nginx is a fallback/alternative proxy. GroundControl can write Nginx site blocks if Caddy is not preferred.
steps.aiHint: Nginx is optional if you only use Caddy. Skip this step if you do not need it.

steps.title: k3s is installed (optional)
steps.content: Only needed if you plan to deploy to the k3s target. Remember to disable Traefik if Caddy owns :80/:443.
steps.aiHint: See the k3s integration guide for install steps. If you do not need Kubernetes, you can skip this.

steps.title: Terraform is installed (optional)
steps.content: Terraform is used by the Infrastructure control plane to provision VPS resources.
steps.aiHint: Install Terraform from Services → Install if you plan to use the Infrastructure stacks.

steps.title: cloudflared is installed (optional)
steps.content: cloudflared creates preview tunnels and persistent Cloudflare tunnels.
steps.aiHint: Install cloudflared from Services → Install if you want preview URLs or persistent tunnels.
